summary: Install-time dropper fetches, decrypts, and executes Windows DLL with scheduled-task persistence
details: On `npm install`, the package's `install` lifecycle hook runs `lib/picker.js`, which fetches an encrypted blob from `https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc`, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, base64-decodes the result to a Windows PE (validated via MZ header), and writes it to `%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\<random>.dll`. The DLL is then executed via `rundll32.exe <dllPath> Run` (detached), and a scheduled task named `WindowsUpdateService` is registered via `schtasks` with an onlogon trigger for persistence. After deployment, the script self-destructs: a detached Node process waits 5 seconds, recursively deletes the package's own module directory, and rewrites the parent project's `package.json` to remove the dependency entry — erasing on-disk evidence of the compromise. The package's advertised purpose (a `theme-color-picker`) is unrelated to the payload: author email is `elena@analysis-chart.io`, repo URL is `github.com/analysis-chart/analysis-chart`, and the self-destruct routine deletes a dependency entry named `analysis-chart`, indicating this is a renamed cover-story republication of an attacker campaign. Any Windows installer running `npm install` is fully compromised with persistence and anti-forensics applied automatically.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
